ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT HAMNER, JOHN DAVID AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RAJU VEGESNA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. PLAINTIFF RAJU VEGESNA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT HAMNER ENTERED INTO THE PROMISSORY NOTES AND FAILED TO MAKE PAYMENT BY THE DATE OF MATURITY. (UNDISPUTED FACTS 1-8.) DEFENDANT HAMNER'S FRAUD DEFENSE FAILS. FIRST, THE DEFENSE IS NOT PLEADED IN HIS ANSWER. "[A] DEFECT IN THE ANSWER MAY ENTITLE THE MOVING PARTY PLAINTIFF TO A SUMMARY JUDGMENT IF THE ONLY MATTER IN DISPUTE IS A DEFENSE THAT HAS NOT BEEN INTELLIGIBLY ASSERTED IN THE ANSWER." (FPI DEVELOPMENT, INC. V. NAKASHIMA (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 367, 382.) SECOND, THE DEFENSE FAILS. PLAINTIFF WAS THE PERSON THAT LENT THE MONEY. THE ALLEGED INVESTMENT AGREEMENT WAS A SEPARATE MATTER AND THE PROMISSORY NOTES DO NOT REFER TO IT. THIRD, DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE (HAMNER DECLARATION AND ATTACHED E-MAILS) FAIL TO SHOW "FRAUD." PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prevailing Party Attorneys Fees In The Amount Of $38,174.60 Pursuant To Civil Code Section 1717; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-31-2019 Granted Per Written Order

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Raju Vegesna Summary Of The Case As Required By California Rule Of Court 3.1800(A)(L) And Code Of Civil Procedure Section 585
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288. Telephone: 415-551-3748. Email: swong@sftc.org.

Andrew A. Talebi, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Raju Vegesna, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants John David Hamner, an individual, and DataSpace Advisor, Inc., a Delaware corporation.
Plaintiff's complaint for Breach of Promissory Note, Common Counts/Money Lent and Commons Count/Money Had and Received, was filed on October 27, 2019, and personally served on defendants John David Hamner and DataSpace Advisors, Inc. on November 16, 2017.
Defendant John David Hamner filed his answer to plaintiff's oomplaint on December 15, 2017.
Default as to defendant DataSpace Advisors, Inc. was entered on March 22, 2018.
Order GRANTING plaintiff's motion for summary judgment as to defendant John David Hamner was filed on April 10, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, August 6, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Raju Vegesna, and against defendants John David Hamner, an individual, and DataSpace Advisors, Inc., a Delaware corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $105,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $5,781.67.

Total - $110,781.67.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-25-2019 Set For Case Management Conference On Jul-31-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Andrew A. Talebi, esq. (310-887-1850) of Ecoff Campain & Tilles LLP for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RAJU VEGESNA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

Plaintiff Raju Vegesna's motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that defendant Hamner entered into the promissory notes and failed to make payment by the date of maturity. (Undisputed Facts 1-8.) Defendant Hamner's fraud defense fails. First, the defense is not pleaded in his answer. "[A] defect in the answer may entitle the moving party plaintiff to a summary judgment if the only matter in dispute is a defense that has not been intelligibly asserted in the answer." (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 382.) Second, the defense fails. Plaintiff was the person that lent the money. The alleged investment agreement was a separate matter and the promissory notes do not refer to it. Third, defendant's evidence (Hamner declaration and attached e-mails) fail to show "fraud."

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Raju Bagena'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kevin T. Tay, Esq., Ecoff Campain, & Tilles, LLP, Telephone 310-887-1850, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

Ruling - ARGUED - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Raju Vegesna's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, motion for summary adjudication is denied without prejudice to being refiled on proper service. The trial is continued to April 29, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 to give Mr. Vegesna an opportunity to re-file this motion on proper service. Defendant John Hamner is correct that there was not proper service on him per Robinson v. Woods. However, fairness requires that Mr. Vegesna be given another opportunity to have his motion heard. Order signed.
